name: Hydrogen
etymology: Derived from the Greek words 'hydro' and 'genes' meaning water-forming.

name: Helium
etymology: Named after the Greek Titan of the Sun, Helios.

name: Lithium
etymology: Derived from the Greek word 'lithos' meaning stone.

name: Beryllium
etymology: Named after the mineral beryl, which contains beryllium.

name: Boron
discoveredBy: Joseph Louis Gay-Lussac, Louis Jacques Thénard
etymology: Derived from the Arabic word 'buraq', meaning borax.

name: Carbon
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'carbo', meaning coal.

name: Nitrogen
etymology: Derived from the Greek words 'nitron' and 'genes' meaning niter-forming.

name: Oxygen
etymology: Derived from the Greek words 'oxys' and 'genes' meaning acid-forming.

name: Fluorine
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'fluere', meaning to flow.

name: Neon
etymology: Named after the Greek word 'neos', meaning new.

name: Sodium
etymology: Derived from the English word soda; the symbol Na is from the Latin 'natrium'.

name: Magnesium
etymology: Named after Magnesia, a district of Eastern Thessaly in Greece.

name: Aluminium
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'alumen', meaning alum.

name: Silicon
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'silex', meaning flint.

name: Phosphorus
etymology: Derived from the Greek word 'phosphoros', meaning bringing light.

name: Sulfur
etymology: Derived from the Latin 'sulfurium' or Sanskrit 'sulvere'.

name: Chlorine
etymology: Derived from the Greek word 'chloros', meaning pale green.

name: Argon
etymology: Derived from the Greek word 'argos' meaning lazy or inactive.

name: Potassium
etymology: Derived from the English word potash; symbol K from Latin 'kalium'.

name: Calcium
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'calx', meaning lime.

name: Scandium
etymology: Named after Scandinavia.

name: Titanium
etymology: Named after the Titans of Greek mythology.

name: Vanadium
etymology: Named after Vanadis, the Old Norse name for the goddess Freyja.

name: Chromium
etymology: Derived from the Greek word 'chroma' meaning color.

name: Manganese
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'magnes' for magnet.

name: Iron
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'ferrum'.

name: Cobalt
etymology: Derived from the German word 'kobold' meaning goblin.

name: Nickel
etymology: Derived from the German word 'kupfernickel' meaning Old Nick's copper.

name: Copper
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'cuprum' meaning from the island of Cyprus.

name: Zinc
etymology: Derived from the German word 'zinke' meaning tooth or prong.

name: Gallium
etymology: Named after Gaul (France), 'Gallia' in Latin.

name: Germanium
etymology: Named after Germany, 'Germania' in Latin.

name: Arsenic
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'arsenikon', meaning yellow orpiment.

name: Selenium
etymology: Named after the Greek word 'selene' meaning moon.

name: Bromine
etymology: Derived from the Greek word 'bromos' meaning stench.

name: Krypton
etymology: Named after the Greek word 'kryptos' meaning hidden.

name: Rubidium
etymology: Derived from the Latin 'rubidus' meaning deep red.

name: Strontium
etymology: Named after the Scottish village of Strontian.

name: Yttrium
etymology: Named after the Swedish village of Ytterby.

name: Zirconium
etymology: Named after the mineral zircon.

name: Niobium
etymology: Named after Niobe, daughter of King Tantalus in Greek mythology.

name: Molybdenum
etymology: Derived from the Greek word 'molybdos' meaning lead.

name: Technetium
etymology: Derived from the Greek word 'technetos' meaning artificial.

name: Ruthenium
etymology: Named after Russia, 'Ruthenia' in Latin.

name: Rhodium
etymology: Derived from the Greek word 'rhodon' meaning rose.

name: Palladium
etymology: Named after the asteroid Pallas, which was discovered at the same time.

name: Silver
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'argentum'.

name: Cadmium
discoveredBy: Karl Samuel Leberecht Hermann, Friedrich Stromeyer
etymology: Named after the mineral calamine, 'kadmeia' in Greek.

name: Indium
etymology: Named after the indigo line in its spectrum.

name: Tin
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'stannum'.

name: Antimony
etymology: Derived from the Latin 'stibium'; the name antimony comes from Greek words meaning 'not alone'.

name: Tellurium
etymology: Named after the Latin 'tellus' meaning earth.

name: Iodine
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'iodes' meaning violet.

name: Xenon
etymology: Named after the Greek word 'xenos' meaning stranger.

name: Caesium
etymology: Derived from the Latin 'caesius' meaning sky blue.

name: Barium
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'barys' meaning heavy.

name: Lanthanum
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'lanthanein' meaning to lie hidden.

name: Cerium
discoveredBy: Martin Heinrich Klaproth, Jöns Jacob Berzelius
etymology: Named after the asteroid Ceres.

name: Praseodymium
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'prasios didymos' meaning green twin.

name: Neodymium
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'neos didymos' meaning new twin.

name: Promethium
discoveredBy: Chien Shiung Wu, Emilio Segrè, Hans Bethe
etymology: Named after Prometheus, who stole fire from heaven in Greek mythology.

name: Samarium
etymology: Named after the mineral samarskite.

name: Europium
etymology: Named after the continent of Europe.

name: Gadolinium
etymology: Named after the mineral gadolinite.

name: Terbium
etymology: Named after the village of Ytterby, Sweden.

name: Dysprosium
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'dysprositos' meaning hard to get.

name: Holmium
etymology: Named after Stockholm, 'Holmia' in Latin.

name: Erbium
etymology: Named after the village of Ytterby, Sweden.

name: Thulium
etymology: Named after Thule, an ancient name for Scandinavia.

name: Ytterbium
etymology: Named after the village of Ytterby, Sweden.

name: Lutetium
discoveredBy: Georges Urbain, Carl Auer von Welsbach
etymology: Named after Paris, 'Lutetia' in Latin.

name: Hafnium
etymology: Named after Copenhagen, 'Hafnia' in Latin.

name: Tantalum
etymology: Named after Tantalus, a figure from Greek mythology.

name: Tungsten
etymology: Derived from the Swedish 'tung sten' meaning heavy stone.

name: Rhenium
etymology: Named after the Rhine river, 'Rhenus' in Latin.

name: Osmium
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'osme' meaning smell.

name: Iridium
etymology: Named after the Greek goddess of the rainbow, Iris.

name: Platinum
etymology: Derived from the Spanish 'platina' meaning little silver.

name: Gold
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'aurum'.

name: Mercury
etymology: Named after the Roman god Mercury; the symbol Hg comes from the Latin 'hydrargyrum'.

name: Thallium
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'thallos' meaning green twig.

name: Lead
etymology: Derived from the Latin word 'plumbum'.

name: Bismuth
etymology: Derived from the German 'weisse masse' meaning white mass.

name: Polonium
etymology: Named after Poland, the homeland of Marie Curie.

name: Astatine
discoveredBy: Dale R. Corson, Kenneth MacKenzie, Emilio Segrè
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'astatos' meaning unstable.

name: Radon
etymology: Named after the element radium.

name: Francium
etymology: Named after France.

name: Radium
etymology: Derived from the Latin 'radius' meaning ray.

name: Actinium
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'aktis' meaning ray.

name: Thorium
etymology: Named after Thor, the Norse god of thunder.

name: Protactinium
etymology: Derived from the Greek 'protos' and actinium, meaning parent of actinium.

name: Uranium
etymology: Named after the planet Uranus.

name: Neptunium
etymology: Named after the planet Neptune.

name: Plutonium
etymology: Named after the planet Pluto.

name: Americium
etymology: Named after the Americas.

name: Curium
etymology: Named after Marie and Pierre Curie.

name: Berkelium
etymology: Named after the city of Berkeley, California.

name: Californium
etymology: Named after the state of California.

name: Einsteinium
etymology: Named after the physicist Albert Einstein.

name: Fermium
etymology: Named after the physicist Enrico Fermi.

name: Mendelevium
etymology: Named after the chemist Dmitri Mendeleev.

name: Nobelium
etymology: Named after the inventor Alfred Nobel.

name: Lawrencium
etymology: Named after the physicist Ernest Lawrence.

name: Rutherfordium
etymology: Named after the physicist Ernest Rutherford.

name: Dubnium
etymology: Named after the town of Dubna, Russia.

name: Seaborgium
etymology: Named after the chemist Glenn T. Seaborg.

name: Bohrium
etymology: Named after the physicist Niels Bohr.

name: Hassium
etymology: Named after the German state of Hesse.

name: Meitnerium
etymology: Named after the physicist Lise Meitner.

name: Darmstadtium
etymology: Named after the city of Darmstadt, Germany.

name: Roentgenium
etymology: Named after the physicist Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen.

name: Copernicium
etymology: Named after the astronomer Nicolaus Copernicus.

name: Nihonium
etymology: Named after Japan, 'Nihon' in Japanese.

name: Flerovium
etymology: Named after the Flerov Laboratory of Nuclear Reactions.

name: Moscovium
etymology: Named after Moscow Oblast, where the element was discovered.

name: Livermorium
etymology: Named after the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory.

name: Tennessine
etymology: Named after the state of Tennessee.

name: Oganesson
etymology: Named after the physicist Yuri Oganessian.
